channel.title: The Mutapa Times — Zimbabwe Stories
channel.description: Packed Instagram-Story autolist — business, property, jobs and internships in one feed. Each item ships with a 1080×1920 story card and a 'Read more on Mutapa Times' CTA.

item.title: SADC countries meet to assess food security in livestock, agriculture, and aquaculture
item.description: via dailynews.co.tz #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: dailynews.co.tz

item.title: ZOU certifies 78 AI educators @bulawayo24
item.description: THE Zimbabwe Open University (ZOU) has certified 78 educators under its pioneering Artificial Intelligence (AI) for Educators Programme, marking a significant milestone in the country’s efforts to mod... via Bulawayo24 News #Zimbabwe #Tech #AfricaTech #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Zimbabwe owes Japan US$381m as debt talks continue @bulawayo24
item.description: ZIMBABWE owes Japan approximately US$381 million in overdue debt, making Tokyo one of the country’s largest Paris Club creditors, Japan’s Ambassador to Zimbabwe, Nobutaka Maekawa, has said.The debt fo... via Bulawayo24 News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: VFEX topples ZSE in dramatic market coup @bulawayo24 @ZSE_ZW
item.description: ZIMBABWE’S youngest capital market, the Victoria Falls Stock Exchange (VFEX), has overtaken the 132-year-old Zimbabwe Stock Exchange (ZSE) in a major structural shift that signals changing investor pr... via Bulawayo24 News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: CIO quizzes Victoria Falls activist @bulawayo24
item.description: THE Central Intelligence Organisation (CIO) has been drawn into controversy after conservation activist Benjamin Larry Norton revealed that intelligence officers held a series of meetings with him ove... via Bulawayo24 News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Bulawayo24 News

item.title: Care Zimbabwe appoints Takawira as first local Country Director
item.description: CARE Zimbabwe has appointed Delilah Takawira as its new Country Director, making her the first Zimbabwean to lead the organisation since it began operating in the country in 1992. Takawira takes up the role with more than two decades of experience in the development sector, inclu via The Zimbabwe News #Zimbabwe #Tech #AfricaTech #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: PPC partners Sinoma to boost Zimbabwe’s cement output
item.description: PPC Limited has signed a Memorandum of Agreement (MoA) with Sinoma Overseas Development Co. Limited to cooperate on initiatives aimed at improving operational efficiencies and expanding production capacity at PPC Zimbabwe (Private) Limited (PPCZ). The agreement, signed on May 19, via The Zimbabwe News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Mtombeni elevated to ZCDC Chief Executive Officer
item.description: THE Zimbabwe Consolidated Diamond Company (ZCDC) has appointed Dennis Mtombeni as Chief Executive Officer. He succeeds Dr Douglas Zimbango in a planned leadership transition aimed at maintaining operational continuity and supporting the company’s long-term growth strategy. Accord via The Zimbabwe News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabweans and other migrants not to blame for SA’s unemployment crisis, says Malema amid xenophobia @EFFSouthAfrica @Julius_S_Malema
item.description: By Simon Majadibodu l IOL ECONOMIC Freedom Fighters (EFF) leader Julius Malema says African migrants are being unfairly blamed for South Africa’s unemployment and failing public services. Malema has labelled the ongoing anti-illegal immigration marches as “Afrophobia” targeting f via The Zimbabwe News #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: City Parking boss bags PhD @zimsituation
item.description: Source: City Parking boss bags PhD –Newsday Zimbabwe The City Parking Board and Management announced yesterday that Muzviyo graduated with a PhD in Business and Management, calling it “a testament to his dedication, intellectual rigor, and visionary leadership.” In a statement, t via Zimbabwe Situation #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: US$94m unveiled to uplift vulnerable households
item.description: Masimba Makwembere WORLD Vision Zimbabwe (WVZ) says it will invest US$94 million under its 2027-2030 strategy aimed at improving the livelihoods of... To access this content, please purchase a subscription by clicking the button below and select a package of your choice! Subscrip via Dailynews #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews
